records.ety: [L. continens, -entis, prop., p. pr. of continere to hold together, to repress: cf. F. continent. See Contain.]

defs: Serving to restrain or limit; restraining; opposing.
Exercising restraint as to the indulgence of desires or passions; temperate; moderate.
quotes: Have a continent forbearance till the speed of his rage goes slower.

defs: Abstaining from sexual intercourse; exercising restraint upon the sexual appetite; esp., abstaining from illicit sexual intercourse; chaste.
quotes: Hath been as continent, as chaste, as true,

defs: Not interrupted; connected; continuous; as, a continent fever.
quotes: The northeast part of Asia is, if not continent with the west side of America, yet certainly it is the least disoined by sea of all that coast.

records.ety: [L. continens, prop., a holding together: cf. F. continent. See Continent, a.]

defs: That which contains anything; a receptacle.
quotes: The smaller continent which we call a pipkin.

defs: One of the grand divisions of land on the globe; the main land; specifically (Phys. Geog.), a large body of land differing from an island, not merely in its size, but in its structure, which is that of a large basin bordered by mountain chains; as, the continent of North America.
notes: ☞ The continents are now usually regarded as six in number: North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, and Australia. But other large bodies of land are also reffered to as continents; as, the Antarctic continent; the continent of Greenland. Europe, Asia, and Africa are often grouped together as the Eastern Continent, and North and South America as the Western Continent.
